title: The Least Privilege Rule 👑
description: Why nobody should be 'admin' by default.

slides.title: Principle of Least Privilege 📜
slides.content:
### PoLP

This is the golden rule of security: **Give people ONLY the access they need to do their job, and nothing more.**

If a janitor only needs a key to the building, don't give them a master key to the CEO's safe!

slides.title: Why it matters? 🛑
slides.content:
### Limiting Damage

If an employee's account is hacked, the hacker only gets the power that account has.

*   **User Account**: Hacker can read emails.
*   **Admin Account**: Hacker can wipe the entire company's database.

quizData.question: An IT intern is given 'Manager' access to the payroll system because it was 'easier than setting up a limited account'. Which principle is being violated?
quizData.explanation: Giving more access than necessary is a direct violation of the Principle of Least Privilege.
